articles.title: The attacks on Sam Altman are a warning for the AI world
articles.summary: A 20-year-old allegedly threw a Molotov cocktail at OpenAI CEO Sam Altman's home after expressing fears about AI-caused human extinction. Altman's residence was targeted again days later, highlighting growing tensions in the AI industry.

articles.title: Chrome now lets you turn AI prompts into repeatable ‘Skills’
articles.summary: Google Chrome now lets users save AI prompts as reusable 'Skills' that can be instantly run across multiple webpages with a single click, eliminating repetitive prompt re-entry.

articles.title: Has Google’s AI watermarking system been reverse-engineered?
articles.summary: A developer claims to have reverse-engineered Google DeepMind's SynthID watermarking system, enabling removal of AI watermarks from generated images or insertion into others, though Google disputes the claim.

articles.title: Anthropic’s rise is giving some OpenAI investors second thoughts
articles.summary: Anthropic's $380 billion valuation appears more attractive to investors compared to OpenAI's recent funding round, which requires assuming a $1.2 trillion IPO valuation to justify.

articles.title: Anthropic co-founder confirms the company briefed the Trump administration on Mythos
articles.summary: Anthropic co-founder Jack Clark explained the company's engagement with the Trump administration regarding Mythos while simultaneously pursuing legal action against the government.

articles.title: Max Hodak’s Science Corp. is preparing to place its first sensor in a human brain
articles.summary: Science Corp. is preparing to implant its first brain sensor in a human. The device aims to treat neurological conditions by delivering electrical stimulation to encourage healing of damaged brain and spinal cord cells.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway, a San Francisco cloud platform with 2 million developers, raised $100 million in Series B funding to build AI-native infrastructure challenging AWS and Google Cloud.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code costs $20-$200 monthly for AI coding features, but Goose offers identical open-source functionality for free with local machine execution.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B funding after a viral billboard hiring campaign in San Francisco. The startup used AI-encoded tokens on the billboard directing talent to a coding challenge, successfully recruiting engineers.

articles.title: “Negative” views of Broadcom driving thousands of VMware migrations, rival says
articles.summary: Nutanix claims to have migrated 30,000 VMware customers amid dissatisfaction with Broadcom's takeover strategy. Customer sentiment remains negative toward Broadcom's VMware management.

articles.title: Iran-linked hackers disrupt operations at US critical infrastructure sites
articles.summary: Iran-linked hackers are disrupting US critical infrastructure by targeting programmable logic controllers in factories, refineries, and water treatment plants. Six US government agencies issued urgent warnings about the advanced persistent threat group's operations since March 2026.

articles.title: Thousands of consumer routers hacked by Russia's military
articles.summary: Russian military's APT28 hacked 18,000-40,000 consumer routers globally to redirect users to credential-harvesting sites for espionage operations.

articles.title: Building trust in the AI era with privacy-led UX
articles.summary: Privacy-led UX treats data transparency and user consent as essential relationship-building elements rather than compliance checkboxes, fostering consumer trust and long-term customer relationships.

articles.title: Redefining the future of software engineering
articles.summary: Agentic AI represents the third major shift in software engineering, enabling autonomous AI agents to manage entire projects end-to-end, moving beyond task-specific assistance to self-directing entities.

articles.title: The Download: the state of AI, and protecting bears with drones
articles.summary: Stanford's AI Index report cuts through conflicting narratives about AI, revealing rapid technological evolution across China-US competition, model breakthroughs, public sentiment, and job impacts.

articles.title: Waypoint-1.5: Higher-Fidelity Interactive Worlds for Everyday GPUs
articles.summary: Waypoint-1.5 enables creation of high-fidelity interactive 3D worlds on standard consumer GPUs. The advancement makes realistic virtual environment generation more accessible to everyday users.

articles.title: Multimodal Embedding & Reranker Models with Sentence Transformers
articles.summary: Sentence Transformers now supports multimodal embedding and reranker models, enabling AI systems to process and compare text with images, videos, and audio simultaneously for improved search and ranking tasks.

articles.title: ALTK‑Evolve: On‑the‑Job Learning for AI Agents
articles.summary: ALTK-Evolve enables AI agents to learn and improve continuously during job execution, adapting to new tasks without retraining. This on-the-job learning approach enhances agent flexibility and real-world performance.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses future directions in artificial intelligence development and its potential impact on technology and society.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels leverage Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and creativity in content creation. The technology enables companies to streamline workflows and generate imaginative marketing materials more efficiently.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft has open-sourced its 'farm of the future' toolkit, making agricultural technology tools available to the public for sustainable farming innovation.

articles.title: Turn your best AI prompts into one-click tools in Chrome
articles.summary: Chrome Skills enables users to discover, save, and remix AI workflows into one-click tools for instant reuse, streamlining repetitive AI tasks.

articles.title: Bringing people together at AI for the Economy Forum
articles.summary: Google hosts an AI for the Economy Forum in Washington D.C., bringing together stakeholders to discuss AI's economic impact and applications.

articles.title: New ways to balance cost and reliability in the Gemini API
articles.summary: Google introduced two new inference tiers for the Gemini API: Flex and Priority, designed to balance cost and latency for developers.

articles.title: Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6: Powering real-world robotics tasks through enhanced embodied reasoning
articles.summary: Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6 enhances autonomous robotics through improved spatial reasoning and multi-view understanding, enabling robots to better perform real-world tasks with enhanced embodied reasoning capabilities.

articles.title: Gemma 4: Byte for byte, the most capable open models
articles.summary: Google releases Gemma 4, its most capable open-source models designed for advanced reasoning and agentic workflows, offering improved intelligence byte-for-byte.

articles.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live: Making audio AI more natural and reliable
articles.summary: Google's Gemini 3.1 Flash Live voice model improves audio AI with enhanced precision and reduced latency, enabling more fluid and natural voice interactions.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #238 - GPT 5.4 mini, OpenAI Pivot, Mamba 3, Attention Residuals
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with 400k-token context windows and claimed token efficiency gains. Mistral open-sourced the Small 4 model family, while discussion covered Mamba 3 and attention residuals in recent AI developments.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #339 - DLSS 5, OpenAI Superapp, MiniMax M2.7
articles.summary: Nvidia launched DLSS 5, using generative AI to enhance video game graphics with real-time photorealism up to 4K by analyzing scene semantics and lighting conditions.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #237 - Nemotron 3 Super, xAI reborn, Anthropic Lawsuit, Research!
articles.summary: Episode 237 discusses major AI developments including Perplexity's local Mac-based AI agent, Anthropic's GitHub PR code review pricing, and new interactive visualization features from ChatGPT and Anthropic.

articles.title: SAP brings agentic AI to human capital management
articles.summary: SAP integrates agentic AI into SuccessFactors HCM to automate administrative tasks across recruiting, payroll, and talent development. AI agents monitor systems, identify anomalies, and resolve data synchronization issues to reduce operational costs and bottlenecks.

articles.title: Canada’s Scotiabank preps for its AI future
articles.summary: Scotiabank launched Scotia Intelligence, an AI framework integrating data and AI operations with governance and security protocols. The platform enables employee access to AI tools while maintaining ethical standards and compliance.

articles.title: Hyundai expands into robotics and physical AI systems
articles.summary: Hyundai Motor Group is expanding into robotics and physical AI systems, planning to invest $26 billion in the US by 2028. The company aims to leverage AI-driven robots for industrial applications as part of its growth strategy beyond vehicles.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to identify which agents cause task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur, improving debugging and reliability of collaborative AI systems.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduces Astra, a dual-model architecture designed to improve autonomous robot navigation in complex indoor environments by addressing localization, self-localization, and path planning challenges.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights using reinforcement learning.

articles.title: The paradox of LLM self-distillation: Faster reasoning, weaker generalization
articles.summary: LLM self-distillation for faster reasoning reduces generalization ability, hindering alternative solution exploration on complex problems.

articles.title: Why harness engineering is becoming the new AI moat
articles.summary: As large language models become commoditized, sophisticated engineering harnesses around them emerge as the true competitive advantage. Anthropic's Claude Code leak demonstrates that the engineering infrastructure, not just the model itself, is the real moat.

articles.title: How GhostClaw malware targets the OpenClaw AI agent boom
articles.summary: GhostClaw malware exploits macOS binaries to steal credentials from developers running local AI agents on Mac Minis, taking advantage of the growing OpenClaw AI agent trend.

articles.title: Google Launches ‘Skills’ in Chrome: Turning Reusable AI Prompts into One-Click Browser Workflows
articles.summary: Google launched Skills in Chrome, enabling users to save frequently used AI prompts as one-click reusable workflows within Gemini. Rolling out April 14, 2026, it represents a convergence of prompt management and browser-level AI agents.

articles.title: A Coding Implementation of Crawl4AI for Web Crawling, Markdown Generation, JavaScript Execution, and LLM-Based Structured Extraction
articles.summary: Tutorial on Crawl4AI framework covering web crawling, markdown generation, JavaScript execution, and LLM-based structured extraction for practical data extraction and automation tasks.

articles.title: TinyFish AI Releases Full Web Infrastructure Platform for AI Agents: Search, Fetch, Browser, and Agent Under One API Key
articles.summary: TinyFish AI launched a unified web infrastructure platform for AI agents, combining search, fetch, browser automation, and agent capabilities under one API key to simplify web interaction tasks.

articles.title: I used the 'Plus Five' rule to fix my iPhone's slow wireless charging - here's how it works
articles.summary: The 'Plus Five' rule helps optimize iPhone wireless charging speed. Users should verify their accessories and setup to achieve faster charging performance.

articles.title: This simple email trick saves me from annoying marketing spam (and it's free to do)
articles.summary: A simple free email technique helps reduce marketing spam and unwanted advertisements in your inbox. The method is easy to implement and often overlooked by most users.

articles.title: A data removal service helped me reclaim my privacy - see if you need one, too
articles.summary: Data removal services automate the deletion of personal information from websites, offering significant privacy benefits beyond manual removal efforts.

articles.title: How to Implement Tool Calling with Gemma 4 and Python
articles.summary: Learn how to implement tool calling with Gemma 4 using Python. This guide covers integrating the open-weights model with function calling capabilities for enhanced AI applications.

articles.title: Structured Outputs vs. Function Calling: Which Should Your Agent Use?
articles.summary: Article compares structured outputs and function calling for AI agents, examining how language models handle text-based inputs and outputs.

articles.title: Beyond Vector Search: Building a Deterministic 3-Tiered Graph-RAG System
articles.summary: A 3-tiered graph-RAG system improves upon vector search by building deterministic retrieval processes. This approach enhances accuracy and reliability in retrieval-augmented generation applications.

articles.title: RAG Isn’t Enough — I Built the Missing Context Layer That Makes LLM Systems Work
articles.summary: RAG systems need a context engineering layer beyond basic retrieval. This Python-based system manages memory, compression, re-ranking, and token budgets to keep LLMs stable under real constraints.

articles.title: Data Modeling for Analytics Engineers: The Complete Primer
articles.summary: Effective data models facilitate asking meaningful questions while preventing poor ones. This primer guides analytics engineers through data modeling best practices.

articles.title: A Practical Guide to Choosing the Right Quantum SDK
articles.summary: A practical guide for selecting appropriate quantum software development kits, helping developers understand which tools to use in different situations and what to avoid.

articles.title: National Robotics Week — Latest Physical AI Research, Breakthroughs and Resources
articles.summary: National Robotics Week highlights AI breakthroughs bringing robots into physical world across industries. Advances in robot learning, simulation, and foundation models enable faster real-world deployment from virtual training.

articles.title: Strength and Destiny Collide: ‘Samson: A Tyndalston Story’ Arrives in the Cloud
articles.summary: Samson: A Tyndalston Story by Liquid Swords is now available on GeForce NOW, allowing players to stream the mythic narrative game on multiple devices via cloud gaming.

articles.title: From RTX to Spark: NVIDIA Accelerates Gemma 4 for Local Agentic AI
articles.summary: Google and NVIDIA optimized Gemma 4 models for local AI execution across devices, from RTX PCs to data centers, enabling on-device agentic AI with real-time context access.

articles.title: Navigating the generative AI journey: The Path-to-Value framework from AWS
articles.summary: AWS introduces the Generative AI Path-to-Value framework to help organizations move beyond proofs of concept and successfully deploy generative AI initiatives to production at scale, addressing technical and governance challenges.

articles.title: Use-case based deployments on SageMaker JumpStart
articles.summary: Amazon SageMaker JumpStart enables quick AI deployment with pretrained models for various use cases to managed inference endpoints or HyperPod clusters, offering deployment options based on concurrent users with performance visibility.

articles.title: Best practices to run inference on Amazon SageMaker HyperPod
articles.summary: Amazon SageMaker HyperPod addresses generative AI inference challenges through dynamic scaling, simplified deployment, and intelligent resource management, reducing total cost of ownership by up to 40%.

articles.title: Building Custom Atomistic Simulation Workflows for Chemistry and Materials Science with NVIDIA ALCHEMI Toolkit
articles.summary: NVIDIA's ALCHEMI toolkit enables custom atomistic simulation workflows for chemistry and materials science, balancing accuracy and computational speed for molecular modeling.

articles.title: NVIDIA NVbandwidth: Your Essential Tool for Measuring GPU Interconnect and Memory Performance
articles.summary: NVIDIA NVbandwidth is an essential tool for measuring GPU interconnect and memory performance in CUDA applications. It helps developers understand data transfer performance in both single and multi-GPU systems.

articles.title: NVIDIA Ising Introduces AI-Powered Workflows to Build Fault-Tolerant Quantum Systems
articles.summary: NVIDIA Ising introduces AI models for quantum processor development, addressing qubit noise through calibration and decoding workflows to build fault-tolerant quantum systems.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: A research study with CrowdDNA examines how generational differences shape consumer attitudes toward advertisements across various social media platforms.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta commits to net zero emissions by 2030, developing carbon mitigation technology and sharing it openly to reduce its environmental impact.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with a non-traditional background developed innovative XR technology by bringing unique perspectives to the field. Their unconventional path proved advantageous in creating cutting-edge solutions.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Research focuses on understanding LLM behavior through feature attribution and interpretability to make AI decision-making more transparent and trustworthy. The study analyzes complex machine learning systems at scale to improve AI safety.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Research presents information-driven design methods for imaging systems that optimize optical encoders to map objects into images while handling noise corruption. The approach uses information estimation to quantify measurement quality.

articles.title: RL without TD learning
articles.summary: A new RL algorithm uses divide-and-conquer instead of temporal difference learning, avoiding TD's scalability issues and performing better on long-horizon tasks.
